articles.title: THEMSELVES
articles.summary: Coding agents stop at code generation. The backend is the part they can't run themselves. InsForge [5] gives them backend infrastructure to run, in the cloud or self-hosted.

articles.title: TALKING TO JSI IN SWIFT: WHAT CHANGED IN SDK 56
articles.summary: Expo SDK 56 has an architectural rewrite for native modules on Apple platforms by allowing Swift to communicate directly with JSI. This change removes the previous Objective-C++ middleware, resulting in performance gains between 1.6 and 2.3 times faster than older versions. By using Swift 5.9's C++ interoperability and non-copyable types, the new system has strict memory safety while simplifying the internal development stack.

articles.title: AI, ASHBY ENGINEERING, AND THE FUTURE
articles.summary: At Ashby, over half of new production code is now AI-generated, yet software quality and stability remain consistently high. Engineers are required to have full accountability for their output by alternating between using AI as a collaborative sidekick for high-stakes decisions and a delegate for low-impact tasks.

articles.title: SITAR-AGENT: BUILDING A RELIABLE DYNAMIC CONFIGURATION SIDECAR AT SCALE
articles.summary: Sitar-agent is a lightweight Kubernetes sidecar for reliably delivering dynamic configuration updates to thousands of service instances without redeployments, using Amazon S3 snapshots for fast bootstrapping and a periodic pull model for changes. The separate sidecar container helps with resource isolation and multi-language compatibility.

articles.title: AI IS NOT A LINE ITEM
articles.summary: Treating AI expenditures as a single, monolithic budget line item based on token usage is a strategic mistake that discourages innovation. Instead, it's better to manage AI investments based on the specific functional value and experimental potential of individual tools.

articles.title: SINGLE RESPONSIBILITY, THE DISTORTED PRINCIPLE
articles.summary: The Single Responsibility Principle (SRP) is often misunderstood as requiring modules to perform a single action. It actually recommends grouping cohesive behaviors under one responsibility.

articles.title: QVAC SDK: RUN LOCAL LLMS, NOW WITH 5X MORE CONTEXT
articles.summary: Run local LLMs, speech, and image models fully on-device with QVAC, an open-source SDK. One npm install, no cloud, no API keys. The latest update integrated TurboQuant, unlocking up to 5x more context from the same machine, near-lossless. Get it on GitHub [13]or see the docs at qvac.tether.io [12].

articles.title: ELIXIR V1.20 RELEASED: NOW A GRADUALLY TYPED LANGUAGE
articles.summary: Elixir v1.20 introduces a gradual, set-theoretic type system that performs automated type inference and checking, using a specialized dynamic() type to accurately identify bugs and dead code. The release also improves compilation speed and allows the compiler to detect potential runtime failures via type narrowing in guards and clauses.

articles.title: BOXES
articles.summary: Boxes provides a cloud-based workbench that allows developers to run multiple AI coding agents in parallel within isolated, persistent virtual machines that can be monitored and managed from any device.

articles.title: WHEN AI BUILDS ITSELF
articles.summary: AI development is transitioning to recursive self-improvement, where autonomous agents accelerate productivity in tasks like coding and research by automating their own refinement. While this offers benefits for discovery and healthcare, it also risks humans losing control.

articles.title: VOIDZERO IS JOINING CLOUDFLARE
articles.summary: VoidZero is joining Cloudflare to speed up the development of foundational web tools like Vite, Vitest, and Oxc. These projects will remain open-source and vendor-agnostic, so developers can continue building portable applications that run on any platform. Cloudflare is also committing a $1 million ecosystem fund to support maintainers and intends to transition its own developer tools to be built directly on top of Vite.

articles.title: USING LOCAL LLMS FOR AGENTIC CODING
articles.summary: Developers can use local LLMs with tools like LM Studio or Ollama for agentic coding to bypass rising cloud costs and improve privacy. Achieving optimal performance requires carefully configuring suitable models and hardware offloading settings, or using services like OpenRouter as an alternative for free hosted models.

articles.title: WHY LEARN PANDAS?
articles.summary: Pandas is a Python library that simplifies data analysis by treating datasets as DataFrames for efficient, loop-free calculations.

articles.title: CHOOSING VALUES FOR ROBUST TESTS
articles.summary: Developing robust software tests requires using non-default, distinct, and varied input values to ensure code logic is correctly executed and to avoid false positives caused by coincidental default values.

articles.title: THEY'RE MADE OUT OF WEIGHTS
articles.summary: Two observers grapple with the revelation that artificial intelligence functions through nothing but numerical weights and matrix multiplication, eventually deciding to dismiss its human-like behavior as mere pattern matching to avoid the ethical implications of its potential sentience.

articles.title: IT IS AN AMAZING TIME FOR PROGRAMMERS!
articles.summary: Modern software developers live in a unique era where they can actively engage with and learn from legendary pioneers of computer science who remain accessible and active in the field.
